content.full_text:
SEAL OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA | California
                                      | Fair Political
                                      | Practices Commission
                                                    April 24, 1990


Iris P. Yang
McDonough, Holland & Allen
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 950
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                        Re:  Your Request for Further Advice
                                             Our File No. A-90-067a

Dear Ms. Yang:

     You are seeking further advice on behalf of the City of Half
Moon Bay and of Councilmember David Iverson regarding his duties
and responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

     In a prior letter, Yang Advice Letter, A-90-067, we advised
that Mr. Iverson could not participate in decisions on the adoption of the proposed redevelopment plan because he owned his place
of residence within the proposed redevelopment plan and any city
council decision on the plan would have a reasonably foreseeable
material financial effect on him or on his real property interest
that was distinguishable from the public generally.

     In your letter of April 3, 1990, you have indicated that
Mr. Iverson has treated the advice letter as preliminary only and
expects a forthcoming formal opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission.  You have therefore requested clarification regarding the procedures of the Commission with respect to advice
letters and opinions.

     In a telephone conversation with Mr. Iverson on April 9,
1990, this office discussed with him the different types of advice
the Commission provides.  Any person with questions concerning his
or her duties under the Act may request the Commission to issue an
opinion (Section 83114(a)) or to provide written advice (Section
83114(b).)

     The prior letter, in which we advised that Mr. Iverson could
not participate in decisions on the adoption of the proposed redevelopment plan, constituted formal written advice pursuant to Sec-

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. A-90-067a
Page 2

tion 83114(b) and Regulation 18329(b). Your letter of January 23,
1990 requested advice pursuant to Section 83114(b).

Mr. Iverson was also informed that he could request Commission review of the prior letter. He has responded with additional
written information and a request for further consideration of his
matter. We have offered to have his letter considered by the Commission at its June 6, 1990 meeting. Until such time as he makes
such a request and the commission concludes otherwise, the advice
provided in A-90-067 remains the advice of the Commission:
Mr. Iverson cannot participate in the redevelopment proposal
decisions.²

I have enclosed copies of Regulations 18320, 18321, 18322,
18324, 18326, 18327, and 18329 for your information. These regulations outline the opinion and advice procedures adopted by the
Commission.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5091.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh

cc: David Iverson

Enclosures


² With respect to matters raised by Mr. Iverson in his letter to
the Redevelopment Agency that you attached to your letter of April
3, the following should be noted. First, there is no forthcoming "formal ruling" of the Commission because the advice provided pursuant to Section 83114 constitutes the advice of the
Commission. Second, the advice provided in A-90-067 was premised
on the fact that Mr. Iverson's residence was located within a
residential area to be included in the proposed redevelopment area
(Regulation 18702.1(a)(3)(D).) As such, decisions on proposed
boundaries create a conflict for Mr. Iverson. Mr. Iverson may
wish to request additional advice when the final boundaries of the
redevelopment area are determined, if new facts arise as a result
of the final boundaries.

In your letter of April 3, 1990, you have indicated that Mr. Iverson has treated the advice letter as preliminary only and expects a forthcoming formal opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission. You have therefore requested clarification regarding the procedures of the Commission with respect to advice letters and opinions. In a telephone conversation with Mr. Iverson on April 9, 1990, this office discussed with him the different types of advice the Commission provides.
